summary: Closed, unmerged grmaterial decomp PR that replaced three function placeholders with C bodies for grMaterial_801C9490, grMaterial_801C9604, and grMaterial_801C9698, updated grMaterial_801C9470 signatures/call forwarding, and added a header include for CommandInfo-related typing. PR body claimed clean ninja build and 100% fuzzy match for the target functions, while the bot report showed 4 new matches but broad unrelated broken/regressed matches; author later said the work was consolidated into other PRs.

key_files.role: Implemented grMaterial_801C9490, grMaterial_801C9604, and grMaterial_801C9698; changed grMaterial_801C9470 and grMaterial_801C9490 signatures to Item_GObj*/CommandInfo*; used Ground user_data, ColorOverlay overlay casts, and raw Ground offsets 0x40 and 0xC0.
Added lb/forward.h include and changed grMaterial_801C9470 prototype from HSD_JObj* to Item_GObj*, CommandInfo*.

what_changed.text: Decompiled grMaterial_801C9490 by reading a u16 command value from cmd->ptr[0], shifting/masking it, storing it as f32 at Ground offset 0xC0, and setting gp->x10_flags.b6.

Decompiled grMaterial_801C9604 by treating Ground offset 0x40 as a ColorOverlay, initializing priority, pointer, timer, loop, and color-enable flags, clearing gp->x10_flags.b4, then calling grMaterial_801C9698.
Decompiled grMaterial_801C9698 by calling lb_80014258 with the gobj, ColorOverlay at Ground offset 0x40, and fn_801C9664 callback; sets gp->x10_flags.b4 when the helper returns true.
Changed grMaterial_801C9470 from an HSD_JObj* wrapper to an Item_GObj*/CommandInfo* wrapper forwarding both arguments to grMaterial_801C9490.
Added lb/forward.h to grmaterial.h, apparently to make CommandInfo or related lb forward declarations visible.

smart_moves.text: The PR updated the public header prototype for grMaterial_801C9470 after changing its recovered signature, rather than only changing the C file.
The author explicitly called out raw pointer math as a known issue and documented why direct field access was not straightforward for the current shared Ground layout.

decomp_lessons.text: When a recovered function signature changes, update both local declarations and owning header prototypes so wrappers and call sites use the same typed arguments.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grMaterial_801C9490 local declaration changed to Item_GObj*, CommandInfo*

decomp_lessons.text: Raw offset access may indicate a real struct-layout gap: this PR's comments identify Ground offset 0x40 as a ColorOverlay overlay region and offset 0xC0 as unnamed padding before GroundVars, so review should distinguish temporary pointer math from a missing union/layout model.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: casts at ((u8*) gp + 0x40) and ((u8*) gp + 0xC0)

decomp_lessons.text: A local target-function 100% match claim is not enough to judge PR health when regression tooling reports broad broken matches elsewhere; keep validation ledgers narrow but also account for blast-radius reports.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: PR body verification claims ninja and fuzzy_match_percent 100.0
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report shows 4 new matches and 42 broken matches plus 21 regressions

naming_conventions.text: Recovered code kept address-style global/function names such as grMaterial_801C9490 and fn_801C9664 where semantic names were not evidenced.
Locals used short role names where roles were clear: gp for Ground*, co for ColorOverlay*, gobj for HSD_GObj/Item_GObj, and cmd for CommandInfo.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used raw byte-pointer offset casts from Ground to access storage at 0x40 and 0xC0, including casting offset 0x40 to ColorOverlay* for initialization and lb_80014258.
For command parsing, read cmd->ptr[0] as u16, shifted right by 2, masked with 0xFF, then converted to f32 before storage.
Used chained assignment to clear two ColorOverlay flag fields in one statement: co->x7C_color_enable = co->x7C_flag2 = 0.

review_feedback.text: Author initially said, 'Will fix pointer math soon.'

Author later explained that the pointer math could not simply be replaced with current struct field access because the material system overlays a ColorOverlay at Ground offset 0x40 and uses offset 0xC0 in unnamed padding; a proper fix would require a non-trivial union around Ground offsets 0x40-0xC3.
Author/comment says the work was 'Consolidated into other PRs.' No review comments or formal reviews were present in the loaded slice.
Bot report recorded new matches for the three grmaterial functions and .sdata2, but also reported many broken matches and unmatched regressions outside the touched file.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that mention 'Consolidated into other PRs' or touch grMaterial_801C9490/grMaterial_801C9604/grMaterial_801C9698 after PR 2249.
Search current Ground struct layout for offset 0x40, offset 0xC0, ColorOverlay overlay, self_vel, cur_pos, and GroundVars union.
Search grmaterial.c history for removal or replacement of ((u8*) gp + 0x40) and ((u8*) gp + 0xC0) pointer math.
Search for CommandInfo and Item_GObj signature updates around grMaterial_801C9470 and grMaterial_801C9490.
Check whether the broad grzebes/grpushon/item regressions from the bot report were real, tooling noise, or fixed by consolidation PRs.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR #2249 fields and changed file counts
human_pr_text: PR body and issue comments
notes: Diff evidence is complete for the two changed files and strongly supports the changed-file summary.
The PR body claims ninja builds clean and fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all functions, but no objdiff log is present in the slice.

Bot report supports that the target grmaterial functions became 100% matched, but also reports broad broken matches/regressions outside the touched files; the slice does not explain whether those were tooling noise, branch baseline issues, or real regressions.
There are no review comments or formal reviews in the loaded evidence.
Pointer-math rationale is from an author issue comment marked '--Claude'; treat as PR-local explanation and source-update candidate, not as an accepted current path fact.
PR was closed with merged_at null and a comment saying it was consolidated into other PRs, so do not treat this PR as merged source truth.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Header prototypes must track recovered signatures
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2249 demonstrates a routine but important matching cleanup: when grMaterial_801C9470/grMaterial_801C9490 signatures changed from HSD_JObj* to Item_GObj*/CommandInfo*, the C declaration, wrapper call, and header prototype were updated together.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Record unresolved typed-field blockers explicitly
accepted_candidate_records.text: The PR leaves raw Ground pointer math but records a concrete blocker: ColorOverlay is said to overlay Ground offset 0x40 and offset 0xC0 is unnamed padding before GroundVars. This is useful intake evidence for later type-layout work, while still remaining temporary from the typed-fields-over-pointer-math standard.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Local matches need regression context
accepted_candidate_records.text: Even when a PR claims clean build and 100% fuzzy matches for target functions, bot reports with broad broken matches should be preserved in the postmortem so curators and reviewers can separate local text progress from unexplained blast radius.

source_update_candidates.title: Potential Ground layout overlay for grmaterial ColorOverlay region
source_update_candidates.text: PR-local comment says grmaterial treats Ground offset 0x40 as a 0x80-byte ColorOverlay overlay reusing the self_vel/cur_pos/padding region, and uses offset 0xC0 in unnamed padding before GroundVars. Curator should verify against current Ground layout and later consolidation PRs before promoting.
source_update_candidates.reason: The comment provides concrete offsets and layout hypothesis, but the PR was unmerged and the explanation is not independently validated in the slice.

source_update_candidates.title: Pointer-math exceptions should name the missing layout model
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding guidance that when raw pointer math remains because a shared struct lacks a known overlay/union, the PR should document the exact offsets, expected overlay type/size, and what structural change would be needed, while routing the layout change to source/type work.
source_update_candidates.reason: This PR illustrates a documented pointer-math blocker; curator should decide whether current standards already cover this sufficiently.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR body's 100% match claim as an accepted validation result without the missing objdiff/build artifacts and without resolving the bot-reported regressions.
Do not promote the Ground 0x40/0xC0 overlay explanation as a current path fact until checked against current source and later consolidated PRs.
Do not infer reviewer intent or acceptance; loaded metadata shows no reviews/review comments and merged_at is null.
Do not treat raw pointer math in this closed PR as preferred style; it conflicts with the typed-fields-over-pointer-math standard unless verified as a necessary temporary bridge.
